source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Growth
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity

Join the team transforming how millions of people access musculoskeletal care by building the engine that powers our growth. As a Staff Software Engineer on the Growth Engineering team, you will not just build features; you will architect the scalable, event-driven backend systems that integrate Hinge Health with major hospital systems and health plans. You will be a technical leader in a cloud-native environment, solving complex integration challenges to expand our ecosystem and deliver life-changing treatments to members worldwide.

What You'll Accomplish

 - In your first 3 months: You will dive into our backend stack (Python, TypeScript, AWS) and audit our current integration architecture. You will build high-trust relationships with product managers and frontend partners, and ship your first set of secure, scalable features to support new growth initiatives.

 - In your first 6 months: You will take ownership of leading architectural discussions for complex, integration-heavy systems. You will design and implement end-to-end solutions using Apache Kafka and DynamoDB, mentor junior engineers to raise the technical bar, and champion improvements in system observability using Datadog and Sentry.

 - In your first 12 months: You will act as a primary owner for the long-term technical strategy of our growth platform. You will have optimized our backend services for massive scale, established durable architectural patterns that multiply the team's velocity, and played a pivotal role in delivering foundational improvements that help us reach 20+ million lives.

Who You Are

 - A "Learn-it-all": You leverage AI tools and emerging trends to drive efficiency and quality. You don't just stick to what you know; you constantly explore new ways to solve hard problems in a cloud-native environment.

 - A Trust Builder: You effectively bridge the gap between backend complexity and frontend experience. You collaborate seamlessly with frontend engineers to define clear API contracts and ensure a cohesive end-to-end user journey.

 - Resilient: You thrive in a dynamic, continuous deployment culture. You take owner-operator pride in your code, proactively identifying scaling bottlenecks and delivering safe, foundational improvements even when facing ambiguity.

Basic Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or related technical degree.

 - At minimum, 4+ years of experience in software engineering, with a proven track record of building scalable backend systems and microservices.

 - 4+ years of experience building event-driven systems, AWS services (e.g., EC2, Lambda, S3, RDS, DynamoDB) and cloud architecture.

 - Strong experience with backend technologies (e.g., Node.js/TypeScript, Python, relational and non-relational databases, messaging/streaming systems).

 - Collaboration skills: Experience collaborating with or contributing to web or mobile frontends (understanding of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and modern frameworks like React, Vue.js, or Angular).

 - AI-Native: Hands-on experience with generative AI tools to accelerate development workflows, improve code quality, and streamline debugging.

Preferred Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience in TypeScript, NestJS, GraphQL, Node.js, relational and non-relational databases, Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, Redis.

 - Integration Expert: Experience designing and evolving integration-heavy systems (e.g., with hospital systems, health plans, or other external partners).

 - Frontend Fluency: Experience with mobile or web frontend development (e.g., React Native, React) sufficient to meaningfully collaborate on end-to-end feature delivery.

 - Observability Champion: Experience with tools such as Datadog (performance monitoring) and Sentry (error tracking).

 - System Design: Strong understanding of system design principles, microservices architecture, and CI/CD pipelines.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Product Manager on Hinge Health’s Intelligent Care team, you will lead the end-to-end experience of our member-facing hybrid care assistant. You will own an LLM-powered conversational product that helps bring high-quality MSK care to wherever our members are. You will own the product strategy, safety, and performance of the assistant’s conversations, ensuring it reaches out to members at the right moments, delivers helpful and clinically sound guidance, and seamlessly escalates to our Care Team when human support is needed.

You’ll collaborate closely with Engineering, Design, Data Science, Clinical, Legal, Compliance, Applied Science, and fellow PMs. We also provide daily breakfast and lunch for onsite team members. Your work will directly shape member experience, drive engagement and retention, reduce care team workload, and advance our company-level strategy for intelligent, scalable care delivery.




WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Establish clear baselines and evaluation frameworks for engagement, conversational success rates, and care team workload reduction.

 - Ship meaningful improvements to the care assistant that measurably impact key KPIs. This includes new use cases, enhanced conversation flows, or safety/guardrail updates.

 - Drive a measurable increase in member engagement by expanding the assistant’s ability to deliver timely, personalized, and high-quality guidance.

 - Achieve reductions in Care Team workload by increasing the percentage of member needs handled safely and effectively through hybrid automation.

 - Improve conversational performance through continuous iteration on prompts, workflows, escalation logic, and quality metrics.

 - Partner with PMs and cross-functional teams to scale the assistant to additional product areas, increasing coverage of member needs and overall program impact.
   
   


HINGE HEALTH HYBRID MODEL

We believe that remote work and in-person work have their own advantages and disadvantages, and we want to be able to leverage the best of both worlds. Employees in hybrid roles are required to be in the office 3 days per week, for the full 8 hours of a typical business day. The San Francisco office has a dog-friendly workplace program.




BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - 2+ years of experience as a Product Manager building and shipping AI-driven software products.

 - Direct experience working with LLM-powered products, including model-driven features, conversational interfaces, or agent-like systems.

 - Familiarity with prompt design, evaluation frameworks, and LLM performance measurement.

 - Experience driving cross-functional initiatives with Engineering, Design, Data Science, and specialized partners (e.g., Legal, Compliance).

 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to use data to define problems, evaluate tradeoffs, and guide decisions.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience with AI safety, guardrails, or human-in-the-loop systems, especially in member- or customer-facing contexts.

 - Background in healthcare, digital health, or regulated environments.

 - Prior work collaborating with clincal teams or subject-matter experts.

 - Experience building member-facing products at scale, particularly those driving engagement, retention, or behavior change.
   
   


ABOUT HINGE HEALTH

At Hinge Health, we’re using technology to scale and automate the delivery of healthcare – starting with musculoskeletal (MSK) conditions, which affect over 1.7 billion people worldwide. With an AI-powered human-centered care model, Hinge Health leverages cutting-edge technology to improve outcomes, experiences and costs to help people move beyond their pain. The platform addresses a broad spectrum of MSK care – from acute injury, to chronic pain, to post-surgical rehabilitation – through personalized, evidence-based care.

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Manager, Direct Mail
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

As a critical member of our rapidly expanding Growth team, you’ll help lay the foundation for a fully scaled, best-in-class marketing organization focused on raising awareness of Hinge Health and driving new member sign-ups. This role will be responsible for leading one of our most significant acquisition channels: direct mail. As the channel owner, you will drive strategy and optimization for our large scale direct mail program. In this highly cross-functional role, you’ll work closely with teams across Accounting, Strategic Finance, Procurement, Data Science, Operations, and Creative. You will lead the efforts of a cross functional team of marketers, designers, operations specialists and data scientists toward a collective goal of achieving topline targets while maximizing channel ROI. You and your cross functional partners will achieve this through analytical rigor, robust experimentation, operational excellence and ongoing innovation.




WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH

 - You’ll focus on building, scaling, and evolving direct mail marketing campaigns for our prospective members, optimizing targeting, mailer formats, content and increasing conversion rates.

 - You’ll manage and allocate a significant marketing budget with precision and accountability, collaborating closely with Finance, Accounting and Procurement to align budgets with topline expectations and ensure cost-effectiveness.

 - You’ll use data and best practices to build a strategy and framework to define and prioritize your efforts, analyzing key metrics (e.g., conversion rates, ROI) and adjust your strategies based on data-driven insights,

 - You’ll identify, secure and manage the internal and external resources needed to fully optimize the direct mail channel to build rapid momentum in the growth and evolution of Hinge Health’s acquisition efforts.

 - You’ll monitor and report on campaign performance, insights and tests results to stakeholders and executive leadership.

 - You’ll stay current with trends in direct mail, consumer behavior, and emerging technologies to continuously refine acquisition strategies.




HINGE HEALTH HYBRID MODEL

We believe that remote work and in-person work have their own advantages and disadvantages, and we want to be able to leverage the best of both worlds. Employees in hybrid roles are required to be in the office 3 days per week, for the full 8 hours of a typical business day. The San Francisco office has a dog-friendly workplace program.




BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3+ years of experience in growth, performance, or direct response marketing; paid channel experience is a requirement, direct mail channel ownership strongly preferred.

 - Demonstrated success driving new customer acquisition using segmentation, personalization and triggered communications.

 - Experience driving impact through creative and copy experimentation, including productive partnerships with design teams.

 - Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data to create actionable marketing strategies.

 - Ability to prioritize projects and handle multiple tasks efficiently.

 - Motivated and energetic, proven ability to work independently, learn quickly and drive toward defined goals and targets.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience overseeing budgets of $10M+.

 - Demonstrated experience working within highly regulated industries (e.g., healthcare, financial services).

 - Experience with large-scale vendor management and contract negotiations.

 - Knowledge of postal regulations, print production processes, or logistics.

 - Previous experience integrating offline (direct mail) and online (digital) channel strategies.

 - Proven success implementing automated direct mail technologies or vendors.

source_jobs.title: Creative Director, Commercial
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THIS JOB

We are seeking a Commercial Creative Director to lead the creative strategy for Hinge Health’s enterprise initiatives, serving as the driving force behind how our brand resonates with the world’s leading employers. In this pivotal role, you will bridge the gap between complex clinical innovation and human connection, partnering with Commercial, Product Marketing, and Sales leadership to transform business goals into high-performing integrated campaigns, bespoke ABM experiences, and immersive brand activations. From overseeing large-scale events, integrated GTM campaigns to sales enablement toolkits, you will lead a talented multidisciplinary team in delivering work that is visually stunning and commercially effective. This is a unique opportunity to combine creative storytelling with business strategy to shape the future of digital healthcare.




WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH



STRATEGIC CREATIVE LEADERSHIP

 - Lead the creative vision: Define and drive the creative strategy for all commercial and B2B marketing initiatives, ensuring lockstep alignment with Hinge Health’s brand identity and business goals.

 - Translate value: Transform complex clinical and technical value propositions into clear, human-centric narratives that resonate with employers, consultants, and benefits leaders.

 - Strategic partnership: Establish a trusted, strategic thought partnership to Commercial, Product Marketing, and Sales leadership, translating business imperatives into high-impact concepts for GTM launches and market expansion.

 - Event experience: Oversee end-to-end creative development for commercial events and activations, seamlessly integrating creative content, digital, analog, and multimedia experiences to ensure a cohesive and immersive attendee journey from promotion to on-site delivery.

 - Performance optimization: Collaborate with Demand Gen to develop performance-driven creative assets, utilizing data and A/B testing to continuously optimize engagement and conversion rates.

 - Operational rigor: Manage creative operations for the commercial function, including agency/freelancer management, project prioritization, resource allocation, and budget oversight.

 - Executive communication: Present creative concepts and strategies to executive leadership, effectively articulating the business rationale and ROI of design decisions.

 - Brand stewardship: Ensure all commercial assets maintain strict brand consistency while adapting effectively to specific channel requirements and audience segments.

 - Future-proofing: Stay at the forefront of B2B marketing trends, effectively integrating new design platforms and generative AI tools to elevate the quality and efficiency of commercial creative work.

 - Experience orchestrating large-scale brand activations, aligning creative

source_jobs.title: Senior Motion Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hinge Health is looking for an experienced Senior Motion Designer to help us evolve and scale our brand through motion. You will produce a wide variety of deliverables—from high-fidelity campaign work and customer stories to product animations and explainer videos. Working within the Brand, Creative & Content team, balance crafting original designs with partnering with Designers, Writers, Producers, and stakeholders to define how our product and brand come to life.

We believe movement is medicine, and you will infuse that belief into our brand expression. We are looking for an A-player who balances deep technical expertise with a "learn-it-all" drive, someone who is passionate about motion graphics, animation and how the story gets translated alongside video editing, and sound design.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Elevate visual storytelling: Concept and craft high-quality design and motion graphics, UI motion, and visual effects that bring the brand narrative to life across video and digital touchpoints.

 - Deliver end-to-end excellence: Drive projects from initial concept to high-fidelity final delivery, utilizing storyboarding, style frames, and animation to execute detail-oriented work within our brand system.

 - Scale motion operations: Evolve our motion principles and guidelines while establishing self-serve workflows and templates that empower the wider organization to produce great work at speed.

 - Champion brand innovation: Push the boundaries of our motion language to deliver innovative work that delights customers and drives measurable business objectives.

 - Optimize for efficiency: Proactively identify and integrate emerging industry trends, software, and tools to continuously improve the quality, speed, and technical capabilities of our motion design output.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Professional Experience: 3 years of experience in motion design, video, and editing.

 - Portfolio: A strong body of work that demonstrates expertise in product, branding, communication design, and storytelling.

 - Production Versatility: The ability to concept, design, and produce motion assets across brand foundations, marketing campaigns, paid social, and organic channels.

 - Design Expertise: Strong conceptual skills with proficiency in motion design, typography, color theory, illustration, photography, and layout.

 - Storytelling: The ability to craft a narrative combined with the production know-how to cut and deliver assets.

 - Systems Thinking: A strategic and detail-oriented approach to building and maintaining design systems.

 - Time Management: The capacity to manage and prioritize multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining high standards and meeting deadlines.

 - Organization: A commitment to maintaining organized systems for project files, assets, and templates.

 - Communication: Clear verbal and written skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders across the organization.

 - Project Management: Excellent organizational skills to drive projects from concept to completion.

 - Technical Proficiency: Expertise in Adobe CC (After Effects), Figma, Final Cut Pro, Cinema 4D, and Rive, along with experience utilizing AI platforms to enhance creativity and efficiency.

 - Business Alignment: The ability to generate creative solutions that directly align with business goals.

 - Mindset: A growth-oriented attitude and comfort with ambiguity in a fast-paced environment.




WHAT SHAPES THIS COMPANY

 - Effective Communication: We are clear, concise and quick to respond.

 - Learn-it-all: We are always willing to learn and get better.

 - Hustle: We are creative and unrelenting, always finding a way.

 - Accountability: We act in the company's best interest and meet commitments.

 - Lead at all levels: We are hands-on, and we set a clear vision.

source_jobs.description:

In this mission-critical role, you will lead the architecture and development of agentic AI systems that deliver personalized pain reprocessing therapy and mindfulness interventions. You will bridge the gap between clinical science and software engineering, building the engine that helps millions of members retrain how their nervous systems process pain.



WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Architect Next-Gen AI Therapies: Build and scale the agentic AI systems that power multi-session conversational therapy, ensuring they maintain context, demonstrate emotional intelligence, and align with clinical protocols.

 - Build a Unified Education Platform: Design the centralized service that delivers personalized pain science education and mindset strategies, adapting content to each member's therapeutic goals.

 - Drive Technical Strategy: Define the long-term technical vision for Brain Health in partnership with product and clinical leadership, balancing immediate delivery with scalable, fault-tolerant infrastructure.

 - Lead & Mentor: Act as a technical beacon for the team, mentoring engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence, and driving cross-functional collaboration across data science, clinical, and platform teams.


WHO YOU ARE

 - A Strategic Builder: You don't just write code; you architect systems that scale. You have a deep understanding of distributed systems and can navigate the complexity of building AI-driven products in a regulated healthcare environment.

 - A "Learn-it-all": You stay on the bleeding edge of AI and software engineering, constantly evaluating emerging technologies to optimize performance and improve the platform.

 - A Cross-Functional Leader: You thrive in multidisciplinary environments, translating complex technical concepts for clinical and product stakeholders to ensure our solutions are both clinically sound and technically robust.

 - An Owner-Operator: You take pride in your work, from architecture to production support, embodying a DevOps culture where you own the reliability and performance of your code.


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

 - Have 4+ years of practical software engineering experience, including meaningful time shipping AI-powered products to real users.

 - At minimum a Bachelor Degree in Computer Science or related field

 - Have led the design and delivery of complex, user-facing products end-to-end — across product surfaces, backend services, and AI/ML systems.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Have worked in early-stage or fast-moving, rapidly evolving environments, and have ideally built products from 0 to 1.

 - Are technically proficient in modern AI application development, with demonstrated depth in working with LLMs (prompt engineering, RAG, agentic systems, evals) and a strong fullstack foundation (e.g., Python, TypeScript/NestJS).

 - Take a product-focused approach to building AI solutions that are robust, safe, and genuinely useful in users' hands.

 - Have successfully driven cross-team alignment to get impactful work shipped, and work with influence over authority.

 - Have experience establishing engineering standards, system architectures, and development best practices — especially around evaluation, safety, and reliability for AI systems.

 - Thrive in fast-paced environments and can navigate ambiguity to deliver high-quality products.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

 - Contributions to open-source AI or software engineering projects.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Hinge Select
source_jobs.description:
The Opportunity Join the HingeSelect team, a high-growth internal startup within Hinge Health that is transforming how our members access care. You will be the technical anchor for our mission to seamlessly connect members with virtual orthopedic specialists and a high-performance network of in-person providers. This is a rare opportunity to operate in a fast-paced 0-to-1 environment while leveraging the resources of a market leader to solve complex challenges in musculoskeletal (MSK) care delivery.

What You'll Accomplish

 - In your first 3 months: You will dive deep into our existing stack (Golang, TypeScript, React Native) to ship critical features for our Specialist Visits product. You will establish initial engineering standards for this new initiative and build trust with cross-functional partners in Product and Design.

 - In your first 6 months: You will lead complex, cross-team initiatives that unlock new product capabilities, guiding the team through ambiguity to deliver durable solutions. You will mentor senior engineers, raising the technical bar and ensuring our clinical journeys are intuitive and high-quality.

 - In your first year: You will drive the architectural vision for our 2026 scale-up plans, designing services capable of handling 100x growth. You will shape the long-term technical strategy, balancing immediate delivery with the reliability and maintainability required for a massive scaling effort.

Who You Are

 - A Strategic Architect: You excel at defining technical approaches for ambiguous problem spaces. You don't just write code; you design scalable systems and APIs (REST or gRPC) that support exponential growth and future product needs.

 - A Force Multiplier: You measure your success by the success of your team. Through mentorship, design guidance, and thoughtful feedback, you enable other engineers to operate more effectively and independently.

 - A "Learn-it-all": You leverage AI thoughtfully to accelerate workflows and improve team effectiveness. You are passionate about our mission and constantly seek new ways to use technology to help people overcome chronic pain.

Basic Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent professional experience.

 - 8+ years of non-internship experience in software engineering, with a proven track record of building scalable backend systems and microservices.

 - Expert proficiency across the full stack (web, mobile, and backend), with deep experience in React.js, React Native, Golang (or similar backend technologies), and TypeScript to drive product decisions.

 - Proven track record of architecting and designing scalable APIs (REST or gRPC) and data models that support exponential growth.

 - Experience leading technical execution in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, working closely with cross-functional partners.

 - Passionate about our mission to help people overcome chronic pain and improve quality of life.

 - Proficiency with AI-assisted development tools (e.g., Cursor, Claude Code) and a passion for leveraging AI technologies to accelerate engineering velocity and design high-quality systems.

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience designing and evolving systems in a rapid-growth, 0-to-1 environment where you navigated ambiguity and iterated quickly.

 - Deep familiarity with our core stack: React Native, React, Node.js, TypeScript, NestJS, GraphQL, Docker, Golang, AWS, Postgres, Temporal, and gRPC.

 - Experience designing and evolving integration-heavy systems (e.g., with hospital systems, health plans, or other external partners).

 - Experience in healthcare, insurance tech, or high-compliance industries (HIPAA, SOC 2).

source_jobs.title: Data Engineering Manager, Data & ML Platform
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Hinge Health is building the data and ML backbone that powers personalized MSK care for millions of members — from real-time product experiences to clinical insights and cost savings for our customers. As a Data Engineering Manager leading our Data & ML Platform team, you’ll sit at the intersection of data engineering, real-time systems, and ML enablement, owning the platforms that make analytics, experimentation, and machine learning reliable at scale. You’ll guide our evolution toward a streaming-first, ML-ready architecture, shaping how data flows consistently across systems and how product and Data Science teams build on top of it — all in service of reducing pain and improving movement for people around the world.

This is not a pure infrastructure or ML engineering role. We’re looking for a data platform leader with strong data modeling instincts, product awareness, and enough ML platform experience to bridge both worlds. Our data platform is maturing and our ML platform capabilities are still early — you’ll make foundational architecture decisions, partner with Data Science to operationalize models, and lead both the team and the technical direction as a tech lead manager.

Hinge Health operates a hybrid model in San Francisco. We believe that remote work and in-person work have their own advantages and disadvantages, and we want to leverage the best of both worlds. Employees in hybrid roles are required to be in the office 3 days per week, for the full 8 hours of a typical business day. The San Francisco office has a dog-friendly workplace program.

 


WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH

In your first 3 months, you will:

 - Deeply understand our current data and ML platform: batch and streaming pipelines, data models, orchestration, and data quality posture across analytics and production systems.

 - Build strong partnerships with Data Science, Product, and other engineering teams; align on top ML and product use cases the platform must unlock.

 - Take ownership of a subset of core pipelines and services, stabilizing reliability and on-call practices while establishing clear SLOs and observability baselines for the team.

In your first 6 months, you will:

 - Lead the evolution of our data platform toward a streaming-first, ML-ready architecture, improving data freshness, consistency, and discoverability across domains.

 - Design and deliver the first iteration of our ML platform layer — feature pipelines, feature store, and model serving patterns — enabling Data Science teams to self-serve within shared governance and operational standards.

 - Drive schema governance and data contracts with upstream service teams to reduce fragmentation, standardize core data models, and improve reliability for downstream analytics and ML consumers.

 - Invest in developer productivity: introduce tooling, templates, CI/CD, and testing practices that make it significantly easier for product and ML teams to build on the platform.

In your first 12 months, you will:

 - Own and evolve the end-to-end data & ML platform strategy, including roadmap, architecture, and operational excellence across streaming, batch, and ML workloads.

 - Partner with Data Science to operationalize models in production — from feature pipelines to serving, monitoring, and retraining — and embed these workflows into our broader data ecosystem.

 - Build, mentor, and retain a high-performing data engineering team, creating clarity of ownership, strong execution habits, and a culture that raises the bar on reliability, scalability, and developer experience.

 - Institutionalize operational rigor (SLOs, incident management, observability, change management) appropriate for a HIPAA/SOC 2–oriented environment, in close partnership with Security and Compliance.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Proactive Communications
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Join the team that is redefining how we connect with our members. Our Proactive Communications initiatives are mission-critical for driving member engagement and health outcomes, moving beyond generic notifications to create delightful interactions that guide members through their recovery journey.

In this role, you will build the intelligence and infrastructure to connect with our members at the right time, with the right message, through the right channel. As a Staff Engineer, you will lead the technical architecture of our unified communications platform, collaborating with Machine Learning Scientists to orchestrate outreach across SMS, email, in-app notifications, and nudges using proprietary data and predictive models.


WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Architect the Communications Engine: Lead the design and scaling of a unified communications platform that orchestrates member outreach across multiple channels (SMS, email, push, in-app) with high throughput and low latency.

 - Drive Technical Strategy: Define and guide the long-term technical roadmap for proactive outreach in partnership with product and engineering leadership, balancing immediate delivery with the flexibility to support future capabilities like Conversational AI.

 - Integrate Intelligent Interventions: Partner with ML scientists to architect systems that integrate proprietary models into communication workflows, ensuring outreach is triggered by predictive member behaviors and needs rather than static rules.

 - Ensure System Reliability & Scale: Optimize system performance to ensure the reliability and deliverability of member-facing messages, maintaining high standards for observability, error handling, and member privacy.

 - Elevate Engineering Practices: Establish and evolve best practices for CI/CD, automated testing, and monitoring for both software services and ML model performance in production.

 - Mentorship & Collaboration: Mentor senior and intermediate engineers, fostering a culture of technical excellence. Build strong cross-functional partnerships across Product, Design, Marketing, and Data Science.

source_jobs.title: Staff Machine Learning Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hinge Health helps people move without pain through digital musculoskeletal (MSK) care. That care only works when members keep doing their exercise therapy, and the right message at the right moment is a large part of what keeps them going.

As a Staff ML Scientist on the Proactive Communications & Notifications team at Hinge Health, you'll own the machine learning that decides what message each member receives, when, and through which channel. At our scale, small gains in relevance and timing compound into large gains in engagement and clinical outcomes.

You'll be the technical leader for ML on the team: setting direction for send-time optimization, propensity modeling, and the experimentation rigor behind every nudge we ship. You'll write code your senior engineers respect, mentor a small ML team, and partner closely with product, data science, and our growth and marketing teams.

Our ideal candidate has shipped recommendation or sequential-decisioning systems that changed how real users behave, runs experiments with rigor, and writes code their engineers respect. They optimize for what moves for members, not model sophistication for its own sake.

 


WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Send-time and channel optimization: Design and ship the next system for deciding what nudge to send a member, when, and through which channel, beyond our current contextual-bandit approach.

 - Propensity modeling: Build and deploy models that decide whether nudging a given member is worth it, balancing engagement against fatigue and unsubscribes.

 - Experimentation rigor: Set the bar for how the team runs experiments: multi-arm tests, sequential testing, CUPED, and guarding against peeking, so our nudge decisions are causally sound.

 - Production ownership: Own at least one model in production end-to-end.

 - Leadership: Mentor the team's ML scientists, guide technical direction, and partner across product, engineering, data science, and the growth and marketing teams.


REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Statistics, Operations Research, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field

 - 7+ years building and deploying ML systems in production at consumer scale

 - At least one recommendation, ranking, or sequential-decisioning system shipped end-to-end (modeling, evaluation, deployment, monitoring, iteration)

 - Fluency in experimentation and A/B testing: multi-arm tests, sequential testing, CUPED, and the common failure modes of online experiments

 - Proficiency in Python and SQL; able to read a colleague's PR and improve it

 - Deep understanding of machine learning and applied statistics


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Contextual bandits or reinforcement learning operated in production

 - Multi-objective optimization (engagement vs. adherence vs. retention vs. cost)

 - Causal inference beyond A/B testing: difference-in-differences, synthetic controls, instrumental variables

 - Cold-start and low-data-regime modeling (healthcare gets thin on per-member data fast)

 - Experience hiring and growing a small ML team

 - Healthcare, fintech, or other regulated-data experience; familiarity with HIPAA and BAA constraints

 - Familiarity with our adjacent stack: Statsig, Databricks, feature stores, Airflow/dbt

 - Familiarity with TypeScript


ABOUT HINGE HEALTH

Hinge Health leverages software, including AI, to largely automate care for joint and muscle health, delivering an outstanding member experience, improved member outcomes, and cost reductions for its clients. The company has designed its platform to address a broad spectrum of MSK care—from acute injury, to chronic pain, to post-surgical rehabilitation—and the platform can help to ease members’ pain, improve their function, and reduce their need for surgeries, all while driving health equity by allowing members to engage in their exercise therapy sessions from anywhere. The company is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager – Vertical Programs
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Hinge Health is looking for a strategic, execution-driven Senior Product Manager to lead our newly forming program launch and optimization squad. This is a high-visibility, high-ownership role at the intersection of product strategy, commercial growth, and member experience, built for someone who thinks like a GM, operates with speed, and knows how to turn ambiguous opportunities into products that deliver tangible user and business value.

In this role, you will own the health of Hinge Health's digital physical therapy program portfolio - consumer-grade products that ultimately land in the hands of our members in a B2B2C model. You’ll scan for the highest-value growth opportunities, build and validate new member-facing program extensions, and establish the operating model for how we launch and optimize programs at scale. You will work closely with commercial, clinical, design, and engineering partners across the org, and will interface regularly with executive leadership on new product bets. This role is for someone who is energized by building something new, comfortable without a defined playbook, and biased toward speed to action.




WHAT MAKES YOU A GREAT FIT

0→1 Experience Shaper
You’ve built net-new, consumer or member-facing products end-to-end, from idea to people actually using them in market (B2C or B2B2C, not just internal tools). You know how to knit together app, service, and hardware touchpoints into one coherent experience — and you think about the full journey, not just the screen.

Strategic Ownership
You operate with a CEO-like sense of ownership, taking complete accountability for your product area's performance and outcomes. You hold member outcomes, business performance, and gross margin in tension simultaneously — and make principled tradeoffs across all three.

Ambiguity Navigator
You thrive in entrepreneurial environments, developing high-quality hypotheses and moving work forward even when success is not yet well-defined.

Bias to Action
You don't need to own a surface to move it. You know how to build the case, earn trust with horizontal teams, and get prioritization decisions made. You default to finding the fastest path to signal, and you know when a decision needs to slow down and build a coalition versus when it needs a prototype live by the next sprint.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

Own Program Portfolio Performance

 - Establish and monitor performance signals across Hinge Health's digital physical therapy programs — engagement, retention, outcomes, and gross margin.

 - Identify where programs are under-optimized and where the expected value of targeted investment is highest.

Build the Launch Playbook

 - Define how the squad operates: how we evaluate new program innovation, handle client customizations, validate product–market fit, and decide when a new program is ready to migrate to horizontal ownership.

Launch New Program Experiences

 - Lead discovery and validation to expand our dPT program portfolio, identifying opportunities to reach new member segments or better engage existing ones using Hinge Health’s core member-facing product foundations.

Deliver Measurable Results

 - Define quantitative and qualitative metrics of success and own the roadmap that drives Hinge Health category expansion.

Shape Priorities Across the Org

 - Collaborate with horizontal product teams and cross-functional partners to ensure program-level priorities get the investment they deserve.

 - Keep stakeholders focused on the highest-value bets and tradeoffs.

Hinge Health Physical Therapists utilize their clinical expertise and knowledge of pain science to deliver care for a large number of patients across the MSK spectrum, including pelvic health, via telehealth video visits and in-app messaging. We believe in providing the best patient-centered care by applying the Biopsychosocial model, evidence-informed practice, and innovative thinking with technology to help patients reduce pain and achieve meaningful outcomes. Through our technology-first platform, Hinge Health physical therapists improve the lives of many at scale. You will work with a diverse set of members within a team of high performing, inclusive clinicians.


Our physical therapists are on the bleeding edge of delivering virtual MSK care enabled by advanced technology. Come join us if you’re excited to evolve alongside the future of PT practice!

source_jobs.title: Health Economist
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Healthcare costs are rising, and employers and health plans need proof that digital MSK solutions deliver measurable savings. As a Health Economist at Hinge Health, you'll be the analytical engine behind our commercial success—designing and executing rigorous ROI studies that demonstrate how our platform transforms member outcomes while reducing costs for over 20 million lives. You'll translate complex claims data into compelling narratives that close deals, inform product strategy, and publish groundbreaking research that positions Hinge Health as the evidence-based leader in digital MSK care.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

In your first 3 months, you will:

 - Build relationships with Client Success, Marketing, and Commercial teams to understand their analytical needs and prioritize high-impact ROI studies

 - Audit existing health economics methodologies and identify opportunities to streamline study designs while maintaining rigor

 - Complete your first client-facing cost analysis, presenting preliminary findings to internal stakeholders

In your first 6 months, you will:

 - Lead end-to-end execution of claims-based ROI studies, including vendor coordination for data acquisition, data transformation in SQL/Python, and analysis using advanced econometric methods

 - Partner with Client Success teams to present study results to external clients (health plan analysts, actuaries), translating technical findings into actionable insights

 - Contribute to clinical study design by identifying key health economics endpoints and measurement strategies

In your first year, you will:

 - Own the health economics research agenda for a specific MSK condition or product line (e.g., chronic back pain, women's pelvic health), acting as the go-to expert for commercial teams

 - Publish at least one peer-reviewed manuscript or conference presentation that elevates Hinge Health's thought leadership in digital health economics

 - Build reusable data pipelines from Hinge Health's claims and EHR warehouse (Databricks) that reduce study turnaround time by 30%




WHO YOU ARE

A Learn-it-all: You stay on the cutting edge of MSK and digital health research, constantly refining your methods and bringing new ideas to the team.

You don't just replicate studies—you improve them.

A Translator: You fluently speak both "data science" and "business," effortlessly moving between technical discussions with actuaries and strategic conversations with commercial leaders.

You know when to prioritize speed over perfection to meet commercial deadlines.

Accountable: You balance multiple competing priorities—ad hoc client requests, long-term research projects, and internal cross-functional needs—without dropping the ball.

You meet commitments and communicate proactively when timelines shift.

Hustler with Rigor: You find creative solutions to data limitations and study design challenges, always delivering defensible, high-quality results that can withstand external scrutiny.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - Graduate degree (Master's or PhD) in health economics, health services research, epidemiology, or a related quantitative field

 - 3-5 years of experience using applied econometric methods (e.g., casual inference, cost-effectiveness analysis, budget impact modeling) to address healthcare cost questions

 - 3-5 years of hands-on experience working with large-scale commercial, Medicare, or Medicaid claims databases, including understanding of medical billing practices and health insurance structures

 - Fluent programming skills in R, SQL, and/or Python for data manipulation, statistical analysis, and visualization




PREFERRED REQUIREMENTS

 - PhD in health economics or health services research

 - 2-5 years of experience with Databricks, Snowflake, or similar cloud-based data platforms

 - Prior publication record in peer-reviewed journals (e.g., Health Affairs, Medical Care, JAMA) or experience presenting at academic/industry conferences

 - Ex

source_jobs.title: Senior Heath Economist
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Digital health is transforming MSK care—but without rigorous economic evidence, innovation stalls. As a Senior Health Economist at Hinge Health, you'll architect the analytical strategy that proves our platform's value to the nation's largest health plans and employers. You'll lead high-stakes ROI analyses, mentor junior economists, and shape the research agenda that informs product development and go-to-market strategy. Your work will directly influence how millions of people access care—and how payers invest in digital health solutions that actually deliver savings.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

In your first 3 months, you will:

 - Assess Hinge Health's current health economics portfolio and prioritize the highest-impact studies for commercial growth and competitive differentiation

 - Establish your presence as a trusted advisor to Client Success and Commercial teams, shaping how we position ROI evidence in sales conversations

 - Lead your first complex external ROI study, including vendor negotiation, study design approval from internal and external stakeholders, and results presentation

In your first 6 months, you will:

 - Own concurrent health economics workstreams across product lines (e.g., chronic MSK, surgical replacement, pelvic health), balancing rigor with commercial timelines

 - Present directly to external clients (health plan C-suite, actuarial teams) with polished, defensible analyses that close deals and renew contracts

 - Mentor a junior health economist, establishing team standards for study design, code review, and client communication

 - Build advanced analytics infrastructure (e.g., automated claims data pipelines in Databricks) that scales the team's capacity

In your first year, you will:

 - Define Hinge Health's long-term health economics research strategy, including publication goals, external partnerships, and methodology innovation

 - Lead publication of high-impact manuscripts in top-tier journals (JAMA, Health Affairs) that establish Hinge Health as the evidence leader in digital MSK care

 - Influence product roadmap decisions by proactively identifying economic opportunities (e.g., "Which member segments drive the highest ROI?") and designing studies to answer them

 - Act as the primary advisor to Senior Leadership Team on health economics questions, shaping how we think about value-based contracting and payer partnerships
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

A Strategic Learn-it-all: You're not just executing studies—you're anticipating what questions the business will need answered in 12 months and building the infrastructure to answer them.

You actively shape team priorities, not just respond to requests.

A Master Communicator: You've presented to C-suite executives, defended methodologies to skeptical actuaries, and published in peer-reviewed journals.

You tailor your message to any audience—from PhDs to sales reps—and always land the key takeaway.

Accountable Under Pressure: You thrive in fast-paced commercial environments where study deadlines directly impact revenue goals.

You make pragmatic trade-offs between perfection and speed, always communicating the implications clearly.

A Leader at All Levels: You're hands-on with code and data, but you also set the vision for how health economics drives business strategy.

You mentor junior team members while influencing senior leadership.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - PhD in health economics, health services research, or a related field strongly preferred; Master's degree with exceptional applied experience considered

 - 6-10 years of experience designing and conducting health economics studies using advanced econometric methods (e.g., causal inference, cost-effectiveness analysis, budget impact modeling)

 - 6-10 years of experience working with large-scale claims databases (commercial, Medicare, Medicaid), including deep knowledge of medical billing, CPT/ICD coding, and health insurance requirements

 - Expert-level programming

source_jobs.title: Demand Generation Manager
source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Hinge Health is on a mission to make our products and programs available to more people in 2026—and we need a strategic operator to help us get there. As our Demand Generation Manager, you'll be the architect behind multi-channel campaigns that fuel our B2B sales pipeline, bringing digital health solutions to millions of people living with musculoskeletal pain. This isn't just about generating leads; it's about building the engine that connects employers, health plans, and partners with care that transforms lives.




HINGE HEALTH HYBRID MODEL

We believe that remote work and in-person work have their own advantages and disadvantages, and we want to be able to leverage the best of both worlds. Employees in hybrid roles are required to be in the office 3 days per week, for the full 8 hours of a typical business day. The San Francisco office has a dog-friendly workplace program.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH


IN YOUR FIRST 3 MONTHS:

 - You'll analyze current paid advertising campaigns (LinkedIn, Google Ads, display) and identify quick-win optimizations to improve lead quality and reduce cost-per-acquisition

 - You'll leverage AI-powered tools (Writer, Anyword, data enrichment platforms) to scale campaign production and improve targeting precision with existing resources

 - You'll take ownership of at least one high-impact campaign from brief to execution, testing messaging and channel mix to establish your baseline performance


IN YOUR FIRST 6 MONTHS:

 - You'll launch a series of targeted account-based marketing (ABM) campaigns in partnership with Commercial Marketing, driving engagement with key accounts

 - You'll optimize paid channel performance, implementing tracking improvements and building dashboards in Google Analytics to measure campaign ROI and pipeline contribution

 - You'll establish a testing framework for continuous campaign optimization, running A/B tests on ad creative, landing pages, and audience segmentation to increase conversion rates by at least 15%


IN YOUR FIRST YEAR:

 - You'll own the demand generation strategy for one or more key segments (employer, health plan, consultant, or ecosystem partners), delivering measurable pipeline growth aligned to business targets

 - You'll act as the primary strategist for paid media investments, managing external relationships and budget allocation to maximize ROI across channels

 - You'll scale what works, creating repeatable playbooks and processes that the broader marketing team can leverage—embodying our "Think Long-Term" principle




WHO YOU ARE

A Data-Driven Optimizer: You don't just launch campaigns—you obsess over the numbers. You know how to track performance across Google Analytics, Google AdWords, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, and ABM platforms, and you use those insights to continuously improve ROI.

A Paid Channel Expert: You have deep expertise in paid advertising (LinkedIn, Google Ads, display, retargeting) and know how to execute campaigns via DSPs. You understand the nuances of audience targeting, bidding strategies, and conversion tracking.

An AI-Augmented Marketer: You see AI as a force multiplier, not a replacement. You're comfortable using AI tools to draft ad copy, enrich lead data, and automate workflows—then applying your strategic judgment to refine and optimize. You stay current on emerging marketing AI capabilities..

A Cross-Functional Collaborator: You've successfully partnered with Sales, Marketing Ops, and creative teams to align campaigns with business priorities. You know how to translate sales goals into marketing strategies and vice versa.

Accountable & Results-Oriented: You set ambitious goals, meet your commitments, and hold yourself accountable to pipeline metrics. When roadblocks arise, you hustle to find solutions.




BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - 5-7 years of B2B demand generation or digital marketing experience, with a proven track record of driving measurable pipeline growth through multi-channel campaigns

We’re looking for a Technical Program Builder to join the Office of the CTO and architect the R&D operational engine. This is a unique “builder” TPM role where you won’t just manage processes. You will design and implement the systems, tooling, and AI agents that automate them.

You’ll leverage AI, automation, and smart infrastructure to eliminate manual coordination, reduce operational drag, and enable teams to move faster with clarity and precision. If you have seen program management at complex organizations first hand and found yourself thinking “this meeting could be automated” or “we shouldn’t need a human to track this,” you’ll thrive here.

This role is about building scalable execution infrastructure - not status reporting. You will act as a force multiplier for engineering teams, creating systems that streamline cross-pod coordination and ensure our internal operations are as innovative as the care we deliver to our members.


WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH


IN YOUR FIRST 3 MONTHS:

 - Audit & Identify: Assess R&D operational processes and identify high-impact opportunities for AI-driven automation and tooling improvements. Create a clean system of processes across R&D.

 - Build Relationships: Establish trust with R&D leadership and pod leads, understanding their pain points and operational bottlenecks.

 - Quick Wins: Deploy initial automated agents or scripts to streamline the most repetitive R&D workflows.


IN YOUR FIRST 6 MONTHS:

 - Launch AI-Driven Infrastructure: Architect and deploy the R&D knowledge base and Internal Developer Portal optimized for both human and AI retrieval.

 - Standardize Operations: Design and roll out standardized operational processes across R&D, championing "AI-native ways of working."

IN YOUR FIRST YEAR:

 - Scale Systems: Build low-code integrations (Jira/Linear, Slack, GSuite) that automate metrics collection and surface project health/velocity in real-time.

 - Transform Execution: Own the end-to-end R&D planning cycle, reducing planning overhead by 30% through automation while improving forecast accuracy.

 - Establish Innovation Culture: Identify and implement AI/ML opportunities that become operational standards, acting as a force multiplier for R&D leadership.


WHO YOU ARE

 - A Builder at Heart: You don't just ask for a tracker; you build the tool that generates it. You are comfortable writing scripts, configuring AI agents, and using technical skills to solve operational problems directly.

 - A Strategic Orchestrator: You excel at "Leading at all Levels," navigating the complexity of cross-pod coordination to align diverse stakeholders on planning and OKRs without losing sight of the big picture. source_jobs.title: Staff Regulatory Affairs Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

The Staff Regulatory Affairs Engineer is the primary regulatory strategist and architect for our AI/ML Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) portfolio. While many companies view Regulatory Affairs as a purely administrative function, at Hinge Health we leverage this role as a technical discipline—hence the "Engineer" title.

In this role, you will be responsible for the hands-on authorship and management of regulatory submissions (510(k), De Novo, Pre-Subs) for our cutting-edge AI products. You will partner closely with Product, Clinical, and Engineering teams to interpret the evolving FDA framework for AI/ML, helping us navigate complex pathways like Predetermined Change Control Plans (PCCP). You will also own the internal regulatory functions that support our Quality Management System, ensuring our rapid innovation remains compliant.



WHAT YOU’LL ACCOMPLISH

 - Submission Leadership (AI/ML SaMD): Lead the planning, authorship, and submission of regulatory filings (510(k), De Novo, Q-Submissions) for AI/ML-enabled software. You will be the primary author, not just a reviewer, translating complex technical and clinical data into compelling regulatory narratives.

 - Regulatory Strategy & AI Policy: Develop and execute regulatory strategies for new product introductions and major software updates. You will serve as the internal authority on the FDA’s evolving AI/ML Action Plan, guiding the business on how to validate and monitor adaptive algorithms.

 - Design Control & Change Impact: Participate in Design Reviews and Change Control Boards (CCB) to assess the regulatory impact of software changes. You will determine when a new 510(k) or "Letter to File" is required, specifically evaluating changes to AI models and retraining pipelines.

 - QMS & Internal Compliance: Support the maintenance of the Quality Management System (QMS) by ensuring regulatory requirements (21 CFR 820, ISO 13485) are reflected in internal procedures. You will act as the Regulatory representative during internal and external audits (FDA, ISO).

 - Promotional Review: Review and approve labeling, marketing materials, and user manuals to ensure claims are substantiated and consistent with cleared indications for use.

 - Post-Market Surveillance & Vigilance: Partner with Quality to monitor post-market performance of AI models. You will lead the assessment of complaints for regulatory reporting requirements (e.g., eMDRs) and manage any necessary recalls or field actions.

 - Cross-Functional Coaching: Educate technical teams on regulatory principles, demystifying the submission process and helping engineers understand how their documentation directly supports regulatory clearance.


BASIC QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Biomedical Engineering, Software Engineering, Regulatory Science, or a related technical/scientific field.

 - Experience: A minimum of 4-6 + years of experience in Regulatory Affairs within the medical device industry, with a minimum of 3 years focused on Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) or Digital Health.

 - Submission Expertise: Proven track record of authoring and clearing US FDA Class II submissions (510(k) or De Novo). Experience interfacing directly with FDA reviewers during the review clock.

 - Technical Fluency: Ability to understand software architecture, cloud infrastructure, and AI/ML concepts sufficiently to describe them to regulators. Familiarity with IEC 62304 and FDA Guidance on Software Validation.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:

 - AI/ML Specifics: Direct experience with regulatory submissions involving Machine Learning, Deep Learning, or Computer Vision. Familiarity with Good Machine Learning Practice (GMLP) principles and Predetermined Change Control Plans (PCCP).

 - International Experience: Experience with international registrations, particularly EU MDR (CE Marking) for software, or Health Canada requirements.

 - Strong expertise with C in a firmware / embedded systems context

 - Proficient with low-level drivers (ADC, PWM, etc.) and communication protocols (I2C, SPI, UART, etc.)

 - Proficient in Python for creating tools for firmware testing and task automation

 - Experience developing in a team environment using version control (Git) and with embedded development tools and workflows (setting up build systems, J-Link, debugging)

 - Familiarity with Electrical Engineering design – You can read datasheets and schematics, or deep dive with an EE to do a pin/peripheral allocation for an MCU

 - Competency with standard lab equipment such as oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, multimeters, and power supplies


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience working in medical device development or, alternatively, within another highly regulated industry (aerospace, automotive, etc.)

 - Experience developing firmware with the help of AI, both as a copilot and in agentic mode

 - Experience developing with an RTOS, especially Zephyr

 - Experience with Nordic nRF5x SoCs and the Nordic Connect SDK

 - Experience with Bluetooth LE

 - Experience with bootloaders, firmware update, and managing memory maps

 - Familiarity with DevOps for embedded – CI pipelines (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI) and automated testing with hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) setups


ABOUT HINGE HEALTH

At Hinge Health, we’re using technology to scale and automate the delivery of healthcare – starting with musculoskeletal (MSK) conditions, which affect over 1.7 billion people worldwide. With an AI-powered human-centered care model, Hinge Health leverages cutting-edge technology to improve outcomes, experiences and costs to help people move beyond their pain.

The platform addresses a broad spectrum of MSK care – from acute injury, to chronic pain, to post-surgical rehabilitation – through personalized, evidence-based care. As the preferred partner to 50+ health plans, PBMs and other ecosystem partners, Hinge Health is available to over 20 million people across more than 2,550 employers. The company is headquartered in San Francisco with additional offices in Montreal and Bangalore.

Learn more at http://www.hingehealth.com

source_jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

At Hinge Health, we're transforming how millions of people access musculoskeletal care—helping them move beyond pain through AI-powered, personalized healthcare. As a Client Executive, you'll be the strategic architect behind our most valuable partnerships, managing a portfolio of complex Enterprise and Jumbo clients who trust us to deliver exceptional outcomes for their members.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives in complexity. You'll manage strategic relationships with enterprise clients, navigating multiple stakeholder groups (e.g. client, consultant, health plan, etc), competing priorities, and high-stakes conversations. Success demands independent problem-solving—when challenges arise, you'll be expected to develop and present solutions rather than simply escalate problems. You'll work across multiple cross-functional teams (Growth, Sales, Data, Marketing, Operations, Product, Clinical) to deliver outcomes, requiring strong project management skills and the ability to influence without direct authority.

The ideal candidate is energized by building executive relationships (internally and externally) and solving complex business challenges. You should be someone who naturally gravitates toward strategic thinking, enjoys high-stakes conversations, and finds fulfillment in turning client challenges into expansion opportunities.




WHAT YOU'LL ACCOMPLISH

Strategic Account Retention
Drive exceptional client retention through proactive relationship management, strategic account planning, and early risk identification across your assigned portfolio of strategic enterprise accounts. Build deep, multi-threaded client relationships that create sustainable competitive advantage and position Hinge Health as an indispensable strategic partner rather than a vendor.

Executive Relationship Management
Build and maintain meaningful relationships per client across different organizational levels. Identify decision-makers, map influence networks, and establish trust with C-suite executives through strategic value conversations that demonstrate measurable business impact.

Independent Strategic Thinking
When facing client challenges, research solutions, analyze options, and present recommendations with data-driven rationale. Facilitate cross-functional problem-solving and drive decisions without constant management oversight, acting as the trusted advisor clients turn to during critical moments.

Revenue Impact Focus
Understand how each client interaction connects to business outcomes (retention, expansion, competitive positioning) and articulate the specific value you're creating through relationship investments. Consistently achieve 100%+ net revenue retention through strategic renewals and expansion opportunities.




WHO YOU ARE

 - A Strategic Relationship Builder: You don't just manage accounts—you architect partnerships. You naturally build trust with C-suite executives and can navigate complex organizational hierarchies with ease.

 - An Independent Problem Solver: When challenges arise, you don't escalate—you investigate, develop solutions, and present recommendations. You thrive in ambiguity and can facilitate cross-functional alignment without formal authority.

 - Resilient Under Pressure: You energize during renewal seasons and high-stakes negotiations. Compressed timelines and competing priorities don't overwhelm you—they motivate you.

 - Data-Driven and Strategic: You translate complex data (utilization metrics, claims analyses, stakeholder feedback) into compelling executive-level narratives that drive purchasing decisions and strategic alignment.


BASIC REQUIREMENTS

 - 10+ years in enterprise account management with demonstrated success managing strategic client relationships. Healthcare, benefits, or SaaS experience strongly preferred.

 - Proven track record of managing strategic enterprise accounts simultaneously while maintaining exceptional retention rates (95%+) in high-growth or competit

notes: High volume of engineering and product roles, with a strong focus on AI and machine learning. Company appears to be rapidly scaling and expanding its product offerings.
